This bar chart illustrates the percentage of registered voters in different age groups who use social media for various voting-related activities. The highest data point is ages 18-29 at 45% for having been encouraged to vote by family and friends, while the lowest data point is ages 65+ at 9% for having encouraged others to vote for a particular candidate. The overall trend shows that engagement with social media for voting purposes decreases significantly as the age of the voters increases. A notable pattern is seen in the "Have announced your vote via social media" category, where ages 18-29 and ages 30-49 show identical engagement at 29%.

This stacked bar chart displays the opinions of different groups of Americans regarding whether climate policies help, make no difference, or hurt the U.S. economy. The highest data point is the Rep/lean Rep group stating policies hurt at 56, while the lowest data point is the Dem/lean Dem group stating policies hurt at 13. There is a significant partisan divide in perspectives, as Democrats largely believe policies help while Republicans largely believe they hurt.
